---
title: "nitrix.smoothing"
generated: true
signature-hash: 13195ee2925f8c58
description: "Edge-preserving and non-edge-preserving smoothing."
listing:
  id: nitrix-smoothing-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-smoothing/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.smoothing`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Edge-preserving and non-edge-preserving smoothing.

This subpackage provides two tiers of smoothing.

- `gaussian` -- separable n-D Gaussian; the unconditional baseline
  for cases where edge preservation is not wanted.
- [`bilateral_gaussian`](nitrix-smoothing-bilateral/bilateral_gaussian.qmd) -- the marquee edge-preserving capability: a
  bounded high-dimensional bilateral filter over a feature-space metric
  (a [`FeatureMetric`](nitrix-smoothing-metric/FeatureMetric.qmd)) and a bounded neighbourhood (grid box, mesh
  k-ring, geodesic ball, or feature-space k-NN). It is built on the
  semiring ELL matrix-multiply primitive as one gather plus one weighted
  reduction, and is clean under `jit` / `vmap` / `grad`. It
  supersedes the retired permutohedral lattice for the feature
  dimensionalities targeted here (and, via a low-rank metric, beyond).

[`susan_emulator`](nitrix-smoothing-susan/susan_emulator.qmd) is a convenience wrapper composing
[`bilateral_gaussian`](nitrix-smoothing-bilateral/bilateral_gaussian.qmd) with the median filter from the `morphology`
subpackage.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-smoothing-symbols}
:::
